name: Zitadel
abstract: Open-source (Apache 2.0) cloud-native CIAM and IAM platform from Switzerland. Designed for multi-tenant B2B SaaS with built-in Organizations for tenant isolation, branding, and per-org login policies. Supports OIDC, SAML 2.0, passkeys, TOTP, and JavaScript-based Actions for extensibility. Available as Zitadel Cloud (SaaS with free tier) or fully self-hosted on any infrastructure. SOC 2 Type II and ISO 27001 certified.

pricing.freeTierLimit: Cloud: 100 Daily Active Users; Self-hosted: unlimited (open source)

featureList.description: Built-in templates for Google, GitHub, GitLab, Apple, Entra ID, Okta, and any generic OIDC or SAML 2.0 identity provider.
FIDO2/WebAuthn passkeys as a first-factor passwordless method. Supports platform authenticators (Face ID, Touch ID, Windows Hello) and roaming security keys.
One-time passcode sent to email for passwordless login.
SMS OTP for passwordless phone-based authentication.
Email OTP can be used as a one-time login link. No dedicated magic-link authenticator out of the box.
Zitadel requires authenticated user identity. Anonymous or guest sessions are not a built-in feature.
Time-based OTP via any TOTP-compatible authenticator app (Google Authenticator, Authy, etc.).
Only TOTP (time-based) is supported. HOTP (counter-based OTP) is not available.
FIDO2/WebAuthn security keys (YubiKey, etc.) as a second factor.
WebAuthn as second factor, supporting both platform authenticators and roaming security keys.
One-time code sent via email as a second factor.
SMS OTP as a second factor.
Single-use recovery codes generated at MFA enrollment for account recovery.
No built-in push notification MFA. Not available as a native feature.
Login Policies allow enforcing MFA at the instance or per-organization level. No risk-based or fully adaptive MFA engine built in.
No native Cisco Duo integration.
Step-up authentication via ACR values and Login Policies that enforce re-authentication or additional factors for sensitive operations.
SAML 2.0 Identity Provider support for enterprise SSO.
Service users with OAuth 2.0 client credentials grant for M2M and backend-to-backend authentication.
Generic OIDC identity provider template for federating with any OpenID Connect provider. Pre-built templates for Google, GitHub, GitLab, Apple, Entra ID, Okta.
SAML 2.0 identity provider federation for enterprise SSO. Pre-built templates for Entra ID and Okta SAML.
LDAP identity provider for federating with Active Directory and LDAP-compatible directories.
Built-in Entra ID (Azure AD) identity provider templates for both OIDC and SAML 2.0 federation.
Bulk import via the admin import API endpoint supporting multiple users, organizations, and associated data simultaneously.
Import users with existing password hashes. Supported formats: bcrypt, argon2, PBKDF2, and MD5 via legacy verifiers. Hashes are transparently re-hashed on first login.
Export users via the management API.
Bulk user update and delete operations via the management and admin APIs.
ImportHumanUser endpoint supports creating or updating existing users during import.
Username constraints depend on instance configuration. No built-in universal legacy username normalization for special characters.
OTP (TOTP) secrets can be imported alongside user accounts during migration. Passkey migration is not supported via direct import.
SCIM 2.0 server for inbound user provisioning. Enables automated user creation, updates, deactivation, and deprovisioning from external IdPs (e.g., Okta). Currently in preview.
Outbound SCIM provisioning (acting as a SCIM client) is on the roadmap but not yet released.
SCIM 2.0 implementation is primarily user-focused. Group synchronization support is limited in the current preview.
Users are automatically created on first login via a federated identity provider without pre-provisioning.
Lazy migration from a legacy user store can be implemented via Zitadel Actions (JavaScript hooks). No built-in lazy migration UI or credential proxy feature.
Built-in self-service account management for end users including profile, MFA device enrollment, and passkey management.
Link external identity providers (social, enterprise, LDAP) to an existing Zitadel user account.
User accounts can be deactivated via the management API or admin console, preventing authentication.
Custom key-value metadata can be stored on user accounts via the management API.
Custom metadata can be stored at the project and application level.
Metadata key and value size limits exist but are not prominently documented for all tiers.
Search and filter users by profile attributes via the management API and admin console.
Role-based access control via project roles assigned to users and organizations. Roles are surfaced in tokens as claims.
First-class multi-tenancy via Organizations. Each org can have isolated users, identity providers, branding, and login policies. Supports B2B SaaS with self-service enterprise federation.
Configurable password complexity policies (minimum length, character requirements) at instance and organization level.
Username format and character restrictions configurable via login policies.
No built-in progressive profiling flow. Custom Actions can trigger additional data collection during login, but there is no native visual form builder.
Attribute-based access control can be built using user metadata and custom claims injected via Actions, but there is no native ABAC policy engine.
No built-in ReBAC or fine-grained authorization engine (e.g., Google Zanzibar-style). Authorization is scope and role-based.
OAuth 2.0 scopes and project roles for API authorization. Supports token introspection for API resource servers.
User data exportable via management API. Immutable event store enables full audit history export.
Users can be fully deleted via the management API to satisfy GDPR right to erasure.
OAuth 2.0 consent screen presented to users during authorization. Consent can be configured per project.
Zitadel Cloud available in EU, US, Switzerland, and Australia regions for data residency requirements.
Self-hosted deployment on any cloud provider (AWS, GCP, Azure) or on-premises via Docker or Kubernetes (Helm chart). Full data sovereignty.
Immutable append-only event store retains all changes and authentication events for an unlimited period.
Event streaming via webhooks and the event API. Supports integration with external SIEM and observability platforms.
No dedicated security center or threat monitoring dashboard. Authentication events are accessible via the event store and admin console.
Data encrypted at rest. Keys managed by the cloud provider for Zitadel Cloud; fully operator-controlled for self-hosted.
All endpoints enforced over TLS 1.2+.
Full customer-managed key control available in self-hosted deployments. Zitadel Cloud does not offer BYOK/customer-managed keys.
No built-in CAPTCHA or ML-based bot detection. DDoS mitigation is provided at the infrastructure level for Zitadel Cloud.
Configurable lockout policy: lock user accounts after a defined number of failed login attempts.
Rate limiting at infrastructure level for Zitadel Cloud. No per-IP suspicious activity detection built into the application layer.
No built-in integration with breach databases (e.g., Have I Been Pwned).
Allowed origins and CORS configuration available at instance level. No IP-based access control lists built into the application layer.
No built-in device fingerprinting or device tracking feature.
Full branding customization (logo, colors, fonts) independently configured per organization via the admin console.
Login Policies configurable per organization, allowing different MFA requirements per tenant.
Centrally hosted login UI managed by Zitadel, fully customizable with branding settings.
No pre-built embedded login component library. Developers can build custom login UI using the Zitadel Session API.
Full white-labeling: custom logos, colors, fonts, and domain. Zitadel watermark can be hidden.
Multi-language support for the hosted login UI and email communications.
SDK quickstarts available for multiple frameworks. No comprehensive pre-built UI component library; custom login UI built using the Session and OIDC APIs.
Admin console shows basic authentication event data. No dedicated analytics dashboard; full event history accessible via the event store API.
Official SDKs and quickstarts for Go, TypeScript/Node.js, Python, Java, .NET, Angular, React, and more.
Full gRPC and REST management APIs for users, organizations, projects, and IAM configuration.
Rate limits enforced on authentication endpoints. Configurable per instance.
Actions: JavaScript code snippets that execute at defined hook points in authentication flows (pre/post authentication, pre token creation, custom claims, etc.).
Actions use JavaScript only. TypeScript is not supported in the runtime.
Custom domain supported on Pro and Enterprise plans for Zitadel Cloud; fully supported in self-hosted.
ZITADEL CLI for managing instances and configuration. Supports GitOps workflows alongside the Terraform provider.
Official Terraform provider (registry.terraform.io/providers/zitadel/zitadel) for managing all Zitadel resources as code.
Zitadel uses its own internal user store. Custom or legacy database connections for credential validation are not supported natively.
Native webhook support via event-based Actions that can push data to external HTTP endpoints on authentication events.
Hosted login page fully customizable with logos, color themes, fonts, and custom text via the branding settings.
Custom SMTP server configuration for sending transactional emails from your own domain.
Fully customizable email templates for OTP, invitation, and notification flows.
Custom claims added to access tokens and ID tokens via Actions pre-token-creation hooks.
No visual drag-and-drop flow builder. Authentication flow customization requires JavaScript Actions code.
No built-in identity verification or document proofing. Can be integrated via custom Actions calling external verification services.
No built-in support for W3C Verifiable Credentials or decentralized identity (DID) standards.
No built-in billing or subscription management integration.
Service users with OAuth 2.0 client credentials grant and JWT profile support machine and AI agent authentication. No dedicated MCP server auth or agentic identity features.
